exemplar.title: What are the effects of active and passive warm-ups on the physical performance and psychological readiness of short-distance competitive swimmers before a competition?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

detailedExemplarData.title: What are the effects of active and passive warm-ups on the physical performance and psychological readiness of short-distance competitive swimmers before a competition?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with clearly defined key terms and consistent focus on short-distance competitive swimming.
The research question is sharply stated, delimited and repeatedly tied back to each section of the investigation.
Frequent signposting and well-linked section headings maintain audience awareness and coherence throughout the essay.
weaknesses: The absence of an explicit methodology section (detailing search strategies, inclusion/exclusion criteria and appraisal procedures) reduces transparency and replicability.
Hypothesis development cites âexisting researchâ without fully clarifying the methods of source selection.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two sub-strands (topic communication and research question) clearly meet the 5â6 band descriptors, demonstrating exceptional clarity, focus and purpose. Although the methodology strand is solid in range and relevance, it falls short of a fully articulated, systematic approach. On balance, the overall performance satisfies the top-band expectations for Focus and Method, meriting the full 6 marks.

strengths: Demonstrates excellent subject-specific knowledge, accurately applying physiological and psychological concepts (ATP-PCr, Bohr effect, Self-Determination Theory, Dual-Mode Model) to the research question.
Terminology is used accurately and consistently, conveying complex biochemical and neuromuscular processes with precision.
Integration of evidence from contemporary peer-reviewed studies underpins and validates analytical claims throughout the essay.
weaknesses: A single anatomical term (âlatissimus dermisâ instead of latissimus dorsi) indicates a minor lapse in technical accuracy.
One discussion point on post-activation potentiation lacks a supporting citation, and one of the three promised psychological theories is not fully integrated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation exhibits consistently high levels of subject knowledge and use of technical terminology, satisfying all descriptors of the top band. Minor factual and citation oversights do not materially undermine the depth or breadth of understanding, justifying the award of the maximum 6 marks.

strengths: Presents a coherent, persuasive argument structure, moving logically from theoretical background through empirical analysis to practical recommendations.
Delivers insightful, actionable critique (e.g., combining active and passive warm-ups, highlighting gender imbalances in sample populations) that demonstrates mature argument development.
Engages critically with source methodology, challenging transferability of land-based studies and identifying statistical and confounding-variable limitations.
weaknesses: Some evaluations (e.g., effect-size reporting, the 39 Â°C warming ceiling) are treated superficially or stated without clear citation.
Occasional redundancy in theoretical background suggests opportunities to streamline and deepen critical focus.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three Critical Thinking sub-strands clearly achieve the highest band (10â12), with strong argument development and critical evaluation. Research analysis, while consistently relevant and thoughtful, occasionally lacks the depth of statistical or methodological interrogation required for a perfect score. The overall performance therefore merits 11 out of 12 marks.

strengths: Follows a clearly appropriate structure with title page, table of contents, introduction, body, conclusion and bibliography laid out in conventional order.
Consistent use of APA-style citations, hanging-indent bibliography, page numbering and word count enhances professionalism.
Dedicated headings for key sections (e.g., âPsychological readiness,â âEvaluationâ) guide the reader effectively and conform to IB conventions.
weaknesses: Table of contents contains misaligned page numbers and omits certain sections (e.g., bibliography).
Inconsistent heading formats, occasional dense paragraphs and minor typographical slips (e.g., mismatched parentheses) slightly impair the polish of the layout.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets all core structural and formatting requirements, with only minor layout and stylistic inconsistencies. These do not significantly detract from readability or navigation, warranting the maximum 4 marks for Presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The student **does engage in genuine critique**, identifying methodological weaknesses (e.g., under-representation of women, lack of aquatic temperature control, unspecified effect sizes) and questioning the transferability of land-based studies to swimming.  

However, some evaluations remain surface-level; statistical robustness, sample sizes and potential confounding variables are rarely probed in depth, and certain pivotal claims (e.g., 39 Â°C threshold) are unreferenced or lightly justified.  

Given the regular but not consistently comprehensive critical stance, the work aligns with the 7-9 band but does not attain the sustained depth required for 10â12.

The research questionââWhat are the effects of active and passive warm-ups on the physical performance and psychological readiness of short-distance competitive swimmers before a competition?ââis explicitly stated, sharply delimited (short-distance, competitive context, two warm-up modalities, dual outcome variables) and revisited in each major section, hypothesis, evaluation and conclusion.  

Every analytical thread (physiological mechanisms, psychological theories, call-room constraints) is explicitly tied back to answering this question, illustrating sustained focus.  

Because the investigation never strays into unrelated themes and constantly frames evidence in terms of its relevance to the question, the strand comfortably meets the 5â6 band for clarity, focus and connection to discussion.

Presentation is generally tidy: a readable font, consistent line spacing, page numbers and a stated word count are provided. APA-style in-text citations and a hanging-indent bibliography are used.  

However, **layout inconsistencies persist**âthe table of contents contains punctuation errors and misaligned page numbers; several sentences are fragmented; and the occasional long, dense paragraph impairs readability. Minor typographical slips (e.g., âlatissimus dermisâ) and uneven heading formats further limit polish.  

Because most but not all layout and stylistic conventions are applied correctly, the work fits the 1â2 band for this strand.

The essay follows the prescribed EE structure (title page, contents, introduction, body, conclusion, bibliography) and is easy to navigate. Nonetheless, **some structural elements are incomplete or missing**: there is no dedicated methodology section; the table of contents omits the bibliography; and several headings are inconsistently formatted.  

While these issues do not render the structure ineffective, they indicate that not all IB organisational conventions have been fully met, placing the work in the 1â2 band for this strand.

The studentâs topic is introduced with precision and purpose from the title page onwards. Key terms such as âactive warm-upâ, âpassive warm-upâ and âpsychological readinessâ are explicitly defined in the opening paragraph, and the focus on short-distance competitive swimming is maintained consistently across the entire essay. Each section heading, from introduction through conclusion, clearly links back to the central inquiry, ensuring that readers are never in doubt about the investigationâs scope.  

Frequent signposting (e.g., separate sub-sections for physiological vs psychological effects, discussion of transition phase regulations, and a dedicated evaluation section) further demonstrates deliberate, audience-aware communication.  

Minor lapsesâsuch as brief detours into long-distance contexts or occasional awkward phrasingârarely obscure meaning and do not detract from the overall clarity. Consequently, the work aligns with the highest descriptor for this strand.

The essay demonstrates **comprehensive and accurate application of SEHS knowledge**. Physiological concepts such as ATP-PCr and lactic energy systems, post-activation potentiation, the Bohr effect and thermoregulatory limits are accurately explained and directly related to sprint-swimming demands.  

Psychological frameworks (Self-Determination Theory, Hedonic Psychology, Dual-Mode Model) are integrated to interpret motivational and affective aspects of warm-up choice, showing sophisticated cross-disciplinary insight.  

Evidence from contemporary peer-reviewed studies (e.g., Racinais et al., Cuenca-FernÃ¡ndez et al., Neiva et al.) is woven into the analysis to substantiate claims. The range and depth of application firmly places the work in the 5â6 band.

Technical language is employed confidently and precisely: terms such as âneuromuscular activationâ, âpost-activation potentiationâ, âaffective stateâ and âtransition phaseâ are used appropriately, and biochemical concepts (e.g., enzyme activity, lactate accumulation) are accurately described.  

Except for a single slip (âlatissimus dermisâ instead of *latissimus dorsi*), terminology is consistently correct, and concepts are explained clearly for an informed audience.  

Because the essay sustains this high level of disciplinary discourse across both physiological and psychological domains, it merits placement in the top (5â6) band for this strand.

The student delivers an **extensive, well-structured analysis** that consistently targets the research question. Comparative evaluation of active vs. passive warm-up modalities is organised around temperature-related, neuromuscular and psychological mechanisms, with evidence synthesised from systematic reviews, experimental studies and sport-specific regulations.  

Data and theory are integrated to explain why certain protocols enhance sprint performance, and limitations (e.g., small female samples, absence of effect-size reporting, land-based bias) are acknowledged.  

Although some statistical critique is light, the depth, breadth and relevance of analysis are convincingly strong, aligning with the 10â12 descriptor for this strand.

The student draws on an impressively large and generally relevant body of primary and secondary literature (â40 sources, including peer-reviewed journals and systematic reviews) and justifies some selections by commenting on participant characteristics or methodological limitations.  

However, the essay still lacks a distinct methodology section outlining search strategies, inclusion/exclusion criteria and critical appraisal procedures. Without this transparency, it is difficult for readers to judge the rigour of source selection or to replicate the process.  

Given the breadth of material and occasional explicit justification of sources, the work exceeds the limited 1-2 band, but the absence of a systematic, clearly articulated method prevents it from reaching the 5-6 band. It therefore fits best in the 3-4 range.

Arguments progress logically from theoretical background to empirical evidence, then to practical implications and recommendations. Each section is clearly signposted, and transitions (e.g., between physiological and psychological considerations, or from analysis to evaluation) generally flow well, creating **a coherent, persuasive line of reasoning**.  

Claims are substantiated with multiple sources, and counter-arguments (fatigue, lactate build-up, possible overheating) are weighed before conclusions are drawn. The discussion culminates in nuanced suggestions for combined warm-up protocols and identifies gaps for future research, demonstrating mature argument development.  

Minor abrupt shifts and occasional redundancy are noted but do not disrupt the overall clarity or focus, so performance sits securely within the 10â12 band.

annotations.comment: Inconsistent dot-leaders and misaligned page numbers in the contents reduce readability; standardize leader lengths and align numerals for a cleaner layout.
The term âlatissimus dermisâ is incorrect; the correct anatomical term is *latissimus dorsi*. Precise terminology is essential for demonstrating accurate subject knowledge.
The Null Hypothesis begins with a lowercase letter, indicating inconsistent formatting. Ensure all section text conforms to standard capitalization conventions.
Theoretical background repeats the research question verbatim, creating redundancy. Streamline content to avoid restating information that has already been covered.
Several references appear more than once (e.g., Born et al. 2022a and 2022b) creating duplication; consolidate entries to streamline the bibliography.
DOI presentation varies across entries; standardize formatting (e.g., âdoi:10.xxxx/xxxxâ) consistently per APA guidelines.
Bibliography entries are not arranged alphabetically by surname; reorder references to meet standard IB and APA conventions.

The topic is communicated with exceptional clarity, precisely specifying active vs passive warm-ups, performance and psychological readiness in short-distance competitive swimming, establishing clear focus and purpose from the outset.
The table of contents omits the bibliography and fails to list all major sections, undermining navigability and adherence to standard extended essay conventions.
Awkward phrasing in the correlation sentence (ââ¦have a correlation with performance outcomes haveâ) disrupts clarity; revise for grammatical correctness and readability.
Restating the research question at the end of the introduction reinforces focus and ensures that readers clearly understand the investigationâs aims.

This overview paragraph alludes to sources without describing the methodology. Include a dedicated methodology section outlining search strategies, inclusion/exclusion criteria and appraisal procedures.
The methodology underpinning hypothesis development is vague, citing âexisting researchâ without clarifying selection or appraisal methods, which limits transparency and replicability.
Excessive hyperlink markup within reference entries disrupts readability; in-text URLs should be shortened or moved to endnotes, with clean APA citations in the list.
A stray fragment appears outside the bibliography (âal_measurement_â¦â), indicating a formatting error; ensure all references are complete and properly placed.
The research question is stated clearly, sharply delimiting modalities, outcome variables, and context, ensuring that all subsequent analysis remains tightly focused on the investigationâs scope.
The dedicated âPsychological readinessâ heading clearly demarcates the shift to mental aspects, enhancing structural clarity and guiding the reader effectively.
The statement on post-activation potentiation lacks a supporting citation; include a specific reference to validate this physiological mechanism.
Only two of the three psychological theories introduced in the introduction are discussed here; integrate the missing Dual-Mode Model analysis for completeness.
Citation style alternates between narrative and parenthetical formats; choose a consistent APA method to improve referencing clarity.
Frequent casual transitions (e.g., âOn the other handâ) reduce academic tone; adopt formal signposts to strengthen coherence and tone.
The section on passive warm-ups lacks its own heading, making it harder to navigate; insert a âPassive warm-upsâ subheading to mirror the active discussion.
The âPhysical performanceâ subheading effectively marks the shift to performance analysis, aiding structural organization and reader orientation.
There is a mismatched parenthesis in the citation â(Racinais, Cocking, and PÃ©riard (2017).â Correct the parentheses to uphold citation precision.
Embedding a full URL within the narrative sentence interrupts flow; relocate links to the bibliography and use parenthetical author-date citations in the text.
The discussion of transition-period regulations could be strengthened by a deeper critique of the source methodology and potential confounding variables.
The 39 Â°C warming ceiling is stated without citation. Add the original Racinais and Oksa (2010) or Hopkins Medicine guideline to substantiate this threshold.
The passive warm-up description spans multiple unbroken paragraphs; reorganize with subheadings or bullet points to improve scannability.
This opening fragment is grammatically incomplete (âneuromuscular system like active warm-upsâ¦â); revise for full sentence structure.
The recommendation to combine active and passive warm-ups is well-reasoned and provides actionable guidance, demonstrating mature argument development.
This energy systems paragraph is overly dense and lengthy; break into shorter paragraphs and consider subheadings to improve readability and focus.
The dedicated Evaluation section follows IB structure conventions neatly, clearly distinguishing evaluative commentary from earlier analysis.
Insightful critique of Cuenca-FernÃ¡ndez et al. for lacking methodological transparency illustrates strong critical evaluation skills.
Highlighting the underrepresentation of women in sample populations effectively exposes a significant research gap for future studies.
The Conclusion heading is clearly formatted and signals closure of argument, supporting overall structural coherence.
The final conclusion succinctly synthesizes how active vs passive warm-ups affect both physical performance and psychological readiness, reinforcing the research questionâs focus.
Repeated material on physiological and psychological mechanisms appears here and earlier; trim redundancy to maintain conciseness and impact.
This final summary integrates technical concepts across domains and clearly articulates the intertwined roles of active and passive methods.
The essay does not include any reflective commentary on the research process or personal learning, missing an opportunity to demonstrate metacognitive engagement.
